sections.context: Records today are anonymous beyond the git commit author. At 50-IC scale this makes contradictions, stale records, and review queues unroutable — there is no one to ping when records conflict, anchors break, or shelf life expires. R-06 is also a hard prerequisite for R-12 (contradiction detection), which has nowhere to send the resolution without an owner. Sequencing list flags this as Next now that R-04 has shipped.
sections.approach: Add `owner` (string, e.g. `@user` or `@team`) and `status` (`draft|active|deprecated`) as built-in optional record fields, then introduce a four-link owner-resolution chain evaluated at `ml record` write time: explicit `--owner` flag → CODEOWNERS lookup against the record's file_anchors/dir_anchors → per-domain `default_owner` → git author fallback. Per-domain `require_owner: true` opts a domain into hard validation (matches the existing `required_fields` enforcement pattern from mulch-3114). Layered on top, ship `ml review` as a read-only command listing records owned by `$USER` that need attention (status=draft, near-expiry, or flagged by doctor). Chosen over alternatives because it reuses the per-domain rules plumbing already shipped, keeps JSONL as the single source of truth, and degrades gracefully when neither CODEOWNERS nor config is configured (git author is always available).

alternatives.name: Single global default_owner only (no resolution chain)
alternatives.rejected_because: Too coarse at org scale — backend records and frontend records have different teams. Per-domain default_owner is the minimum viable granularity.

alternatives.name: Owner stored externally (e.g. .mulch/owners.yaml mapping IDs to handles)
alternatives.rejected_because: Breaks JSONL-as-source-of-truth and creates a second file to keep in sync. Records carry their own owner inline.

alternatives.name: Resolve owner only at read time (e.g. during prime/review)
alternatives.rejected_because: CODEOWNERS changes over time; resolving lazily means the same record reports different owners on different days. Stamp once at write time; let `ml doctor` recompute on demand if needed.

alternatives.name: Skip CODEOWNERS, use only --owner + default_owner + git author
alternatives.rejected_because: CODEOWNERS is already the canonical org ownership map for files. Bypassing it forces orgs to duplicate that mapping in mulch.config.yaml.

steps.title: Schema: add owner and status as built-in optional record fields
steps.description: Add `owner: string` and `status: 'draft' | 'active' | 'deprecated'` (default `active`) to the base record schema in src/schemas/record.ts and the generic AJV factory. Both are optional and additive — existing records without these fields remain valid. Update src/utils/expertise.ts dedup logic to ignore status when computing dedup keys (so flipping draft → active does not create a duplicate). Add tests covering round-trip read/write/prime.

steps.title: Config: add domains.<name>.require_owner and domains.<name>.default_owner
steps.description: Extend DomainConfig in src/utils/config.ts with optional `require_owner: boolean` (default false) and `default_owner: string`. Validate at config load time: require_owner only meaningful if owner is allowed by allowed_types, default_owner must look like a handle (`@<name>`). Update mulch.config.yaml init wizard examples. Tests in test/utils/config.test.ts.

steps.title: CODEOWNERS parser utility
steps.description: Add src/utils/codeowners.ts implementing GitHub's CODEOWNERS format: blank lines and `#` comments ignored, lines are `<glob> <owner1> <owner2>...`, last matching pattern wins. Honor canonical lookup paths in order: `CODEOWNERS`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `docs/CODEOWNERS`. Read-only utility — no caching beyond a single load per command invocation. Export `resolveOwners(filePath: string, repoRoot: string): string[]`. Tests cover globs, comments, blanks, missing files, multi-owner lines.

steps.title: Owner resolution chain at ml record write time
steps.description: In src/commands/record.ts, after schema validation and before file write: if no --owner flag provided, walk the chain — CODEOWNERS lookup against each file_anchor/dir_anchor (first match wins) → domain.default_owner → git author email. Stamp the result onto the record. If domain.require_owner is true and the chain produces no result (impossible with git author fallback unless we are not in a git repo), exit non-zero with a clear hint pointing at --owner / CODEOWNERS / default_owner. Tests in test/commands/record.test.ts.

steps.title: ml review command: list records owned by $USER needing attention
steps.description: New src/commands/review.ts (read-only, no locking needed). Default scope: records where owner matches `$USER` (resolved from git config user.email or --owner override) AND any of: status=draft, classification=tactical with age > 10 days, classification=observational with age > 25 days, or doctor reports flagged anchors/domain violations. Flags: `--owner <handle>`, `--status <draft|active|deprecated>`, `--json`. Joins the read-only command tier (prime/query/search/status/validate/learn/ready/rank). Tests cover scope, filters, and json shape.

risks: CODEOWNERS parser edge cases — glob patterns differ subtly from gitignore, last-match-wins is the opposite of most lookup tables. Mitigate with a fixture-based test suite mirroring github.com/octokit's CODEOWNERS reference cases.

Existing records have no owner. Two options: (a) leave them unowned and let ml doctor surface the gap, (b) grandfather by stamping git-blame author on first read. Pick (a) — explicit migration is safer than silent backfill, matches how dir_anchors[] and extends: were rolled out.

Owner migration when files move: file_anchors may resolve to a different CODEOWNERS owner after a refactor. Decision: do not auto-recompute on move; ml doctor adds a check that reports drift, leaving the fix to the human owner.
@team handles vs @user handles — no validation in v1; treat as opaque strings. ml doctor can later add a CODEOWNERS-membership check.
Performance: CODEOWNERS lookup runs on every ml record. Parse once per invocation, cache in-process. ml record latency budget is already tight; benchmark against a 1000-line CODEOWNERS file.
Interaction with extends: <builtin>: custom types extending built-ins must inherit the new owner/status fields automatically. Verify in test/utils/registry.test.ts.
acceptance: owner and status round-trip through ml record → JSONL → ml prime without loss; existing records without these fields remain valid (backward compatible).
ml record in a domain with default_owner: '@platform-team' and no --owner flag stamps owner='@platform-team' on the record.
ml record in a domain with require_owner: true and no resolvable owner exits non-zero with a hint that names the missing chain links.
CODEOWNERS lookup correctly resolves a file_anchor or dir_anchor to its owner; multi-owner lines return all handles; missing CODEOWNERS file is non-fatal (chain falls through).
ml review --owner @foo lists records owned by @foo with status=draft or near-expiry classifications; --json emits structured rows with id/domain/type/owner/status/reason.
All 1120 existing tests still pass; new tests cover schema, config, CODEOWNERS parser, resolution chain, and ml review.
ROADMAP.md R-06 entry updated with a Shipped: line citing the seed id and date, mirroring R-01/R-04/R-05a/e/f format.

sections.context: Stress-testing PR #21 (release v0.8.0, 58 commits, 10 features) surfaced 20 distinct findings across two adversarial agents and one migration/E2E agent. Existing 1120-test suite passes; these are gaps the unit tests don't cover. Two are critical data-integrity bugs (silent duplicate-ID writes via ml restore; A↔B supersession cycles archive both records). Several others are HIGH-severity UX failures (hook timeout reports kill but CLI hangs; raw Bun stack traces from config typos; recipe loader accepts non-default exports despite spec). The dogfood branch (now merged) exercises 4 of the 10 features in mulch's own .mulch/ — finding #14 and #15 along the way. Bundling these into a single hardening pass keeps the v0.8.x line stable without re-cutting major releases per fix.
sections.approach: Group the 20 findings into 7 logically cohesive child seeds by code path / failure surface, prioritized P0→P3. Each child seed bundles related fixes that touch overlapping code (e.g. all three recipe loader bugs live in src/commands/setup.ts and src/utils/recipe-discovery.ts) so a single PR / test run / review naturally covers them. Critical fixes (restore dup-ID, supersession cycle) ship together because both are archive-lifecycle data integrity. Polish/UX nits batch into one trailer issue. No cross-step blocking dependencies — each child can land independently as soon as a contributor picks it up.

alternatives.name: File 20 individual issues
alternatives.rejected_because: Too granular; many fixes share files and tests, and 20 P0–P3 issues with no chaining is hard to prioritize at a glance. User explicitly asked to batch.

alternatives.name: Single monolithic 'v0.8.1 hardening' bug with 20 checkboxes
alternatives.rejected_because: Loses sd's per-issue lifecycle (assignment, status, close). Hard to parallelize across contributors.

steps.title: P0: Archive-lifecycle data integrity (ml restore dup-IDs + supersession cycles)
steps.description: Fix #1: ml restore appends without checking live expertise for the same id, silently creating duplicates that doctor's content-based dedup misses (src/utils/archive.ts:116). Fix #2: A→B + B→A circular supersession demotes both records every prune pass; both archive within 3 cycles, no cycle detection. Add pre-restore live-id scan with cross-domain-ambiguity-style error; add DFS cycle skip in supersession demotion. Test cases for both. Both are CRITICAL data-loss / corruption bugs.

steps.title: P1: Hook reliability — timeout actually kills + mutable-events doc/code drift
steps.description: Fix #3: hook with timeout_ms: 500 + sleep 60 reports timeout in error message but CLI hangs the full 60s. Cause: Promise.all over Response(stdout/stderr).text() blocks on pipe EOF, which doesn't fire after SIGKILL of sh because the descendant sleep keeps the pipe open. Fix by awaiting proc.exited first, then reading captured streams with a short fallback, or by using detached spawn. Fix #19: runHooks docstring says pre-record/pre-prime/pre-prune are mutable but only pre-record + pre-prime are in MUTABLE_EVENTS — align doc to code. HIGH severity — undermines documented hook safety contract.

steps.title: P1: Provider recipe loader hardening (throw, named exports, --list shadow)
steps.description: Fix #4: recipe install() that throws (sync) or rejects (async) crashes CLI with raw Bun stack — wrap recipe invocation in src/commands/setup.ts:767 with try/catch + 'Recipe X failed: ...' framing. Fix #5: TS recipe with no default export silently accepted because mod.default ?? mod falls through to module namespace; spec says 'must default-export' — explicit check for default presence. Fix #11: --list shows both .ts and .sh entries with the same name as separate rows with no shadow indicator on the loser; show a single row with a shadow flag matching the existing builtin-shadow pattern. All three live in setup/recipe-discovery code. HIGH severity.

steps.title: P1: Config error surfacing — wrap registry init + harden doctor against minimal configs
steps.description: Fix #6: errors thrown by buildRegistryWithCustomTypes (src/registry/init.ts:32) for disabled_types/aliases/extends/custom_types misconfig bubble as raw Bun stack traces with source excerpts. Wrap at CLI top level with formatted config-error output. Fix #9: ml doctor crashes (TypeError: undefined is not an object) when YAML omits governance or classification_defaults blocks (doctor.ts:176, 491) — defensive defaults or schema-required enforcement at readConfig. Fix #17: custom_types entry without dedup_key AND without extends crashes every command at validateCustomTypeConfig instead of failing config validation cleanly — graceful error matching the rest of the validator's style. HIGH severity — every config typo currently produces frightening output.

steps.title: P2: dir_anchors normalization + project-root containment
steps.description: Fix #8: './src/utils' is not normalized to 'src/utils', so dedup misses and a record can store ['./src/utils', 'src/utils'] as separate entries. Extend normalizeDirAnchor (src/utils/dir-anchors.ts:3) to strip './' prefix in addition to handling '' and '.'. Fix #7: --dir-anchor /etc and --dir-anchor ../../escape are accepted; resolve(root, '/etc') discards the prefix and returns /etc, so existsSync passes — abs paths and traversal bypass project-root scoping. Reject absolute paths; reject paths that resolve outside the project root. Both fixes touch the same util. MEDIUM severity.

steps.title: P2: Custom types polish — name regex docs, field types, alias collisions
steps.description: Fix #14: custom_types name regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ rejects hyphens; neither CHANGELOG nor init-scaffold says so. Add a doc note to CHANGELOG + the optional-knobs scaffold comment + improve the registry error to name the constraint. Fix #15: all custom-type Ajv field props typed as { type: 'string' } in src/registry/custom.ts:283-289 — flake_rate: 0.05 fails AJV, must stringify. Allow per-field type declaration in custom_types config (e.g. { flake_rate: { type: 'number' } }) while keeping string the default. Fix #16: alias whose legacy_name matches another type's base field (e.g. [name]) is accepted at init; the rewrite only fires for the owning type, but a misclassified record gets silent field renames — reject at config validation. All three are custom-type registry layer. MEDIUM severity.

steps.title: P3: Polish batch — migration wording, archive parse errors, anchor grace days, rank flag parsing, --quiet semantics
steps.description: Fix #10: 'Migration is automatic for legacy configs' implies on-disk migration of domains: string[] → Record<string, DomainConfig>, but writeConfig is only called from add/delete-domain. Either migrate-on-write across all write commands or update CHANGELOG / docs to clarify the in-memory shim. Fix #13: malformed archive line crashes search --archived / restore with raw 'JSON Parse error' — no file:line pointer. Match the targeted 'Unknown record type at file:line (id=...)' error pattern. Fix #12: grace_days: 0 decays records on the same calendar day they were recorded (uses fractional ageDays > graceDays). Either round to whole days or use >= so 0 means 'don't decay anything within the same calendar day'. Fix #18: ml rank --limit '1xyz' parses as 1, --min-score '1.5xyz' parses as 1.5 — parseInt/parseFloat lenient; reject with clear error. Fix #20: --quiet suppresses the disabled_types deprecation warning, silently writing a deprecated record type — counter-intuitive, document or surface a one-line summary even under --quiet. LOW priority docs/UX cleanup batch.

risks: Critical fixes (#1, #2) need careful test coverage so we don't paper over the symptom. Cycle detection via DFS must handle long chains and self-loops without quadratic blow-up.
Hook timeout fix (#3) interacts with the spawn API surface — naive 'kill harder' approaches can race against fast-exiting hooks. Validate against the existing hooks-integration test suite.
Recipe loader changes (#4–#5) could break already-shipping recipes that rely on the lenient default-export fallback. Audit the example recipes (examples/recipes/) and any known npm package consumers.
Custom types per-field type support (#15) is the largest scope item — risks expanding into a feature creep refactor. Keep it minimal: number, boolean, string only; skip enum/regex/union for v1.
Migration wording fix (#10) is partly docs and partly behavior — pick one and ship; do not ship half-on-disk migration.
acceptance: All 7 child seeds closed; tests added/updated in each child PR.

1120-test suite still passes; new regression tests cover at minimum: ml restore over duplicate live id, A↔B supersession cycle prune pass, hook timeout with subprocess that holds stdout open, recipe with no default export rejected, recipe install() throw produces formatted error, config missing governance block produces formatted doctor output (not crash), dir_anchor normalization for './prefix' and rejection of absolute / traversal paths, custom_types hyphenated name produces named-constraint error, alias collision with foreign-type base field rejected at init.
CHANGELOG.md updated with a [0.8.1] section listing each fix with seed id + one-line summary.
ml doctor on the dogfooded .mulch/ config (now merged) continues to pass after each fix lands.
